name: Jeff Hamilton
abstract: Jeff Hamilton (born August 4, 1953) is an American jazz drummer and co-leader of the Clayton-Hamilton Jazz Orchestra. A former member of the L.A.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: Diana Krall, Vocals and Piano
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Nicki Parrott
about_quotes.evidence: if I’m at a gig say, and I remember being on the gig once with Jeff Hamilton out in California... Now when I was playing with Jeff
about_quotes.doc_title: Nicki Parrott interviewed by Monk Rowe. Utica, New York, November 6, 2010 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: I was in this band from the first rehearsal... John puts a lot of stuff on Jeff and on Jim... they all three handle the band very good
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: Jeff Hamilton played with us for a while. He was a great drummer for our band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Jeff Hamilton is a fabulous drummer, by the way. We all know that. When I see him now, I say, “Don’t get too good, because you’re good enough. You’re better than good now. Stop it.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: From what I heard, I never heard that much, but he was perfect for that type of trio. Jeff Hamilton used to play drums for them occasionally. Jeff is a fine drummer by the way.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: And it was an amazing band. It was Ray Brown and Jeff Hamilton played the first year on drums... And we went on the road for three months.
